data.title: Rex American Resources Signals Growth After Record EPS
data.description:
Rex American Resources ((REX)) has held its Q4 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Rex American Resources’ latest earnings call struck an upbeat tone, underscoring record profitability, robust cash reserves, and strategic progress on both capacity expansion and clean-fuel incentives. Management acknowledged some volume softness and higher expenses in the quarter, but emphasized that record EPS, strong gross margins, and 45Z tax credits leave the company well positioned despite regulatory and cost headwinds.

Record Ethanol Volumes and Sales Momentum

Consolidated ethanol sales reached a record 290,000,000 gallons in fiscal 2025, edging above the prior year’s 289,700,000 gallons. Management tied the gain to solid export demand and generally favorable industry conditions, framing the volume strength as a base for future growth once new capacity comes online.

Net Income Surge and All-Time High EPS

Net income attributable to shareholders jumped to $83,000,000 from $58,200,000, a gain of about 43%. Diluted EPS hit an all-time high of $2.50 versus $1.65, while fourth quarter EPS leapt to $1.32 from $0.31, signaling powerful year-end operating leverage.

Q4 Gross Profit Strength Despite Modest Full-Year Gain

Fourth quarter gross profit climbed to $28,900,000 from $17,600,000, an improvement of roughly 64%. For the full year, gross profit ticked up to $93,700,000 from $91,500,000 as better ethanol pricing and lower corn costs offset other pressures.

45Z Tax Credits Add a New Profit Tailwind

The company recognized about $28,000,000 of 45Z tax credits in fiscal 2025, which management estimated at roughly $0.10 per gallon across total volumes. These credits are expected to run through 2029 and could rise materially if carbon capture and sequestration comes online, representing a key earnings lever.

One Earth Expansion Positions Capacity for 2026

REX reported that expansion work at its One Earth Energy plant is nearing completion, set to lift annual capacity to 200,000,000 gallons. Testing and commissioning are expected with the aim of full operation in fiscal 2026, which would enhance both volume growth and eligibility for clean-fuel incentives.

Growth in Corn Oil and Modified Distillers Grains

Corn oil sales rose to roughly 97,000,000 pounds from 88,100,000 pounds, about a 10% increase, with fourth quarter volumes up around 7%. Modified distillers grain volumes also expanded, reaching 81,900 tons versus about 70,000 tons a year earlier, underscoring growth in high-value co-products.

Cash-Rich Balance Sheet and No Bank Debt

The company ended fiscal 2025 with $375,800,000 in cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments, up from $359,100,000. With zero bank debt outstanding, management stressed its financial flexibility to fund expansion and carbon capture projects without straining the balance sheet.

Disciplined Capital Deployment on Growth Projects

To date, REX has invested about $166,000,000 in carbon capture and ethanol expansion initiatives. The company remains within its overall project budget range of $220,000,000 to $230,000,000, signaling disciplined capital allocation as it builds out future earnings drivers.

Long Profit Streak and Supportive Market Backdrop

The company has now posted 22 consecutive profitable quarters, highlighting consistent execution through cycles. Management pointed to strong export demand, healthy corn supplies that help manage feedstock costs, and a constructive policy backdrop, including ongoing 45Z support and potential E15 developments.

Softness in Dry Distillers Grains Volumes

Dry distillers grains volumes slipped to 612,000 tons from 632,000 tons, a decline of roughly 3%. Fourth quarter volumes were about 151,000 tons, down around 9%, suggesting some demand or mix headwinds in this particular co-product line.

Quarterly Ethanol Volume Weakness Amid Record Year

Despite the full-year record, fourth quarter ethanol volumes declined to 70,100,000 gallons from 74,600,000 gallons. Management framed the weakness as seasonal or timing-related, but it nonetheless highlights some near-term volume variability in an otherwise strong year.

Rising SG&A and Incentive-Driven Q4 Spike

Selling, general, and administrative expense increased to $32,600,000 from $27,100,000, up about 20% year over year. Fourth quarter SG&A nearly doubled to $12,300,000 from $6,200,000, mainly due to higher incentive bonuses tied to the company’s strong profitability.

Lower Interest and Other Income

Interest and other income fell to $15,000,000 from $19,200,000, a drop of roughly 22%. While not central to the operating story, this decline reduced a helpful ancillary income stream compared with the prior year.

Pre-Tax Income Edges Lower Before Credits

Income before taxes and noncontrolling interest slipped to about $88,600,000 from $92,900,000, a decline of roughly 5%. This modest drop reflects some margin pressures before the benefit of 45Z tax credits, highlighting the growing importance of policy incentives to overall earnings.

Carbon Capture Permitting Delays Cloud 45Z Upside

Carbon capture and sequestration permits, including the Class VI injection permit and related pipeline approvals, remain pending with federal and state regulators. A revised timeline into September adds uncertainty, and management identified permitting as a key swing factor for unlocking additional 45Z upside.

Guidance: Expansion, Credits, and Another Strong Year

Looking ahead to 2026, management expects continued strong performance, driven by the One Earth capacity increase, ongoing 45Z credits, and favorable market fundamentals. The company anticipates a profitable first quarter and another year of solid earnings, while carbon capture remains a potential catalyst that could further enhance per-gallon economics once permitted and operational.

Rex American Resources emerged from its earnings call with a clearly positive narrative: record EPS, robust cash, and strategic projects that could meaningfully lift future returns. While regulatory timing, expense growth, and some product softness present challenges, investors heard a story of a financially strong ethanol producer leaning into growth, policy incentives, and cleaner fuel economics.

CXApp Inc. issued an aggregate 10,028,891 shares of common stock to Avondale Capital, LLC under two pre-paid purchase arrangements linked to a March 26, 2025 securities purchase agreement. The stock was issued in a series of private transactions on February 17, 23, March 2, 13, 19 and 23, 2026 at prices ranging from approximately $0.1568 to $0.1994 per share, relying on an exemption for non‑public offerings under the Securities Act of 1933, which underscores the company’s continued use of private capital-raising structures and has dilutive implications for existing shareholders while strengthening its funding base.

These issuances reflect CXApp’s strategy of securing financing through negotiated pre-paid share purchases with a single institutional investor, rather than conducting a broader public equity raise. The structure may provide funding flexibility and speed for the company, while concentrating ownership exposure for Avondale and signaling to the market that CXApp is willing to accept lower per‑share prices over time in exchange for committed capital.

data.title: Enzon Completes Merger and Rebrands as Viskase Holdings
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Enzon Pharmaceuticals ( (ENZND) ) has provided an announcement.

On March 26, 2026, Enzon Pharmaceuticals, Inc. completed an all-stock merger in which Viskase Companies, Inc. merged into a wholly owned subsidiary and subsequently converted into Viskase Companies, LLC, with the combined public parent renamed Viskase Holdings, Inc. As a result of the deal, former Enzon shareholders own about 45% of the combined company and former Viskase shareholders about 55%, with Enzon’s former preferred stock held by Icahn affiliates exchanged into 5,658,396 common shares ahead of closing.

The merger was accompanied by a reverse stock split and the company’s common stock began trading on a split-adjusted basis on the OTCQB under the temporary symbol ENZND for 20 trading days, reflecting a significant recapitalization and change in market identity. Governance shifted as Viskase’s chief executive Thomas D. Davis became CEO of the combined group, a new seven-member board led by chair Robert Flint was installed, and legacy Enzon directors and its special committee stepped down, consolidating control with the Viskase leadership and formalizing the transition away from Enzon’s historical pharmaceutical focus.

On the corporate structure side, the company filed amendments in Delaware to effect the name change to Viskase Holdings, Inc. and to eliminate the unused Series A-1 Junior Participating Preferred Stock, returning those shares to authorized but undesignated preferred status. The IEH share exchange and related issuance relied on private offering exemptions under U.S. securities law, highlighting the transaction’s capital structure implications for major stakeholders, including Icahn-affiliated holders and the new majority Viskase shareholder base.

More about Enzon Pharmaceuticals

Viskase Holdings, Inc., formerly Enzon Pharmaceuticals, Inc., is the publicly traded parent of Viskase Companies, LLC, a leading global producer of non-edible cellulosic, fibrous and plastic casings used to prepare and package processed meat and poultry products. Viskase also provides value-added product support services to major global consumer products companies in the food sector.

Following the all-stock merger completed on March 26, 2026, the combined company is focused on advancing Viskase’s meat and poultry packaging business under the Viskase Holdings name and continues to have its common stock traded on the OTCQB market under a temporary ticker symbol.

Average Trading Volume: 826

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: $299.1M

Find detailed analytics on ENZND stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: CEA Industries Pressures 10X to Reform Asset Agreement
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

CEA Industries ( (BNC) ) has shared an update.

On March 26, 2026, CEA Industries Inc. provided an update on its Board’s efforts to renegotiate its August 2025 Asset Management Agreement with 10X Capital Asset Management. The company said it proposed “market” amendments on February 18 and March 8, including cutting management fees from 1.75% to 0.50% of treasury NAV plus a potential performance bonus, shortening the term from 20 years to two, and substantially reducing liquidated damages on termination, which it considers far more favorable to stockholders than terms sought by YZi Labs.

According to the Board, 10X has not delivered a comprehensive counterproposal or substantive feedback on key economic terms and has only offered a nominal fee cut that would still leave it better off after the termination of a side agreement with YZi Labs. The Board accused 10X of stalling through a separate benchmarking exercise, stressed that it cannot unilaterally amend or terminate the restrictive agreement without a large break fee, and said it will continue pursuing ways to reform the AMA to enhance shareholder value amid an ongoing consent solicitation battle with YZi Labs.

The most recent analyst rating on (BNC) stock is a Hold with a $3.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on CEA Industries stock, see the BNC Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on BNC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, BNC is a Neutral.

The score is primarily supported by strong TTM cash generation and a very low-debt balance sheet, but it is held back by weak core operating profitability and multi-year inconsistency. Technical indicators are notably bearish (below major moving averages with negative MACD), while valuation looks optically cheap but is tempered by earnings-quality concerns. Corporate events add uncertainty due to an emerging governance/control dispute.

To see Spark’s full report on BNC stock, click here.

More about CEA Industries

CEA Industries Inc., which trades on Nasdaq under the ticker BNC, is a growth-oriented company focused on building category-leading businesses in consumer markets. The company’s strategy includes building and managing what it describes as the world’s largest corporate treasury of BNB, positioning it at the intersection of consumer-focused ventures and digital-asset treasury management.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

Cantor Equity Partners I, Inc. Class A ( (CEPO) ) has shared an update.

On March 25, 2026, CEPO, BSTR Holdings, Inc. (Pubco), BSTR Newco, LLC and BSTR Holdings (Cayman) amended their July 16, 2025 business combination agreement to expand Pubco’s post-closing board from five to seven members, or another mutually agreed size. The change comes as the parties advance a broader set of proposed transactions, including a business combination and private placements involving unregistered securities, with proxy materials and a Form S-4 registration statement to be filed with the SEC for CEPO shareholders to vote on the deal and related matters.

The transactions, if completed, would take Pubco public and fund its Bitcoin-focused business strategy, but they remain subject to numerous closing conditions, shareholder approvals and market risks, including potential high redemption levels, listing uncertainties and the inherent volatility and regulatory complexities of Bitcoin and crypto assets. CEPO, Pubco and Newco, whose directors and officers may solicit proxies for the deal, warn that the outcome of the combination and the expected benefits for investors are uncertain and could be significantly affected by legal, competitive, tax and operational challenges after the merger closes.

More about Cantor Equity Partners I, Inc. Class A

Cantor Equity Partners I, Inc. (CEPO) is a Cayman Islands exempt company structured as a special purpose acquisition vehicle focused on effecting a business combination. Through its proposed merger with BSTR Holdings, Inc. and related entities, it is positioning to create a publicly traded platform centered on Bitcoin-related advisory and other Bitcoin-focused services, exposing investors to the highly volatile digital asset sector.

Skyline Builders Group Holding Ltd. Class A ( (SKBL) ) has provided an announcement.

On March 20, 2026, Skyline Builders Group Holding Limited entered into agreements for a private placement of $16.575 million in senior unsecured convertible notes and $600,000 in Series B preferred shares, which closed on March 25, 2026. The securities, issued under Regulations D and S, are convertible into Class A ordinary shares at $2.40 per share, subject to anti-dilution protections and a $1.50 floor, providing the company with $17.175 million in gross proceeds for working capital and general corporate purposes.

Placement agents Dominari Securities and Ocean Wall received cash fees and warrants exercisable at $2.40 per share, and all officers and directors agreed to a 180-day lock-up on share sales following the later of the closing or registration effectiveness. Skyline also granted registration rights to investors and the intermediaries, committing to file a registration statement for the resale of conversion and warrant shares, a move that bolsters near-term liquidity while setting up potential future equity dilution and signaling active capital markets engagement to fund its operations.

More about Skyline Builders Group Holding Ltd. Class A

Skyline Builders Group Holding Limited (Nasdaq: SKBL) is a Hong Kong-based civil engineering contractor focused on public works, primarily roads and drainage projects. The company typically serves as a subcontractor, though it is fully qualified to act as a main contractor on both public infrastructure and private residential and commercial developments in Hong Kong.

Radian Group ( (RDN) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On April 30, 2025, Radian Mortgage Capital LLC entered into a Master Repurchase Agreement with Everbank Bank, N.A. to finance its acquisition of residential mortgage loans and related assets, supported by a guaranty from parent Radian Group Inc. The facility enables RMC to fund loans held for sale to investors or for securitization, with subsequent amendments since April 2025 updating pricing terms.

On March 23, 2026, Radian and Everbank executed a second amendment to the repurchase agreement, reducing the maximum borrowing capacity to $25 million while leaving all other terms, including the parent guaranty, unchanged. The revision narrows RMC’s available leverage under this facility but maintains the existing structural and credit support framework for its mortgage loan financing activities.

The most recent analyst rating on (RDN) stock is a Hold with a $39.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Radian Group stock, see the RDN Stock Forecast page.

These positives are partially offset by weak technical signals (below key moving averages with negative MACD) and execution/visibility risks discussed on the earnings call around the Inigo integration, near-term liquidity reduction, and higher defaults.

data.title: Jack Henry Expands Revolving Credit Facility to $1 Billion
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Jack Henry & Associates ( (JKHY) ) has shared an update.

On March 25, 2026, Jack Henry & Associates entered into a new five-year, $1.0 billion unsecured revolving credit facility, replacing its prior $600 million revolver that was scheduled to mature in 2027. Roughly $80 million outstanding under the old agreement was refinanced into the new facility at closing, with no early termination penalties incurred.

The expanded facility can be used to refinance existing debt, fund capital expenditures, repurchase equity, and support general corporate purposes, giving the company greater financial flexibility and firepower for growth initiatives. The agreement includes variable-rate pricing tied to Term SOFR or an alternate base rate, customary covenants and events of default, leverage and interest coverage tests, as well as the option to increase commitments, and is guaranteed by the company’s material domestic subsidiaries, underscoring a robust but disciplined capital structure.

data.title: Hallmark Venture Group Delays Annual SEC Filing
data.description:
Hallmark Venture Group ( (HLLK) ) has released a notification of late filing.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Hallmark Venture Group, Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25 to notify investors of a delay in submitting its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025. The company’s annual filing is late, but all other required SEC reports over the past year have been submitted on time.

The company cites an incomplete audit of its financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2025 as the sole reason for the delay. Management states that the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) could not be completed by the original deadline without incurring unreasonable effort or expense while the audit work remains outstanding.

Hallmark Venture Group indicates that it expects to file the delayed Form 10-K (Yearly Report) within the standard 15-day extension window allowed under SEC rules. Investors should therefore anticipate that the annual report will be submitted no later than mid-April 2026, assuming the audit is finalized as planned.

The company has flagged that it anticipates a significant change in results of operations compared with the prior fiscal year, once the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) is filed. Detailed figures and full financial statements will only be available in the completed filing, and any expectations at this stage should be treated as forward-looking and subject to change when the audit concludes.

Hallmark Venture Group emphasizes that it is working to complete the audit and bring its reporting fully up to date in line with SEC requirements. The notification was signed on March 26, 2026 by Paul Strickland, the company’s Principal Financial Officer, underscoring executive-level responsibility for the delayed filing and ongoing compliance efforts.

More about Hallmark Venture Group

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

data.title: Biophytis Showcases AI-Driven Longevity Drug Discovery with LynxKite at NVIDIA GTC 2026
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Biophytis ( (BPTSY) ).

Biophytis SA, a clinical-stage biotech focused on age-related diseases and longevity therapeutics, is advancing its lead candidate BIO101 for muscular and metabolic indications. The company operates from Paris with international subsidiaries and maintains listings in both Paris and on the U.S. OTC market, targeting the expanding global longevity and muscle health sectors.

From March 16 to 19, 2026, Biophytis and partner LynxKite Technologies participated in NVIDIA’s GTC 2026 conference, a major global forum for artificial intelligence and accelerated computing. Their joint presence, supported by Nebius AI Cloud, highlighted an AI-driven drug discovery platform for longevity, showcased BIO101 and the MAS receptor in a live demo, and included meetings with investors and industrial partners, underscoring Biophytis’ ambition to sit at the forefront of the $1.5 trillion longevity market.

The collaboration between Biophytis, LynxKite and Nebius combines aging biology expertise, graph-based AI and scalable GPU cloud infrastructure to accelerate modeling of biological targets. By engaging with technology leaders and potential collaborators at GTC, Biophytis signaled a strategic push to integrate advanced computational tools into its R&D, potentially strengthening its competitive position in AI-enabled longevity drug discovery.

The most recent analyst rating on (BPTSY) stock is a Hold with a $0.65 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Biophytis stock, see the BPTSY Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on BPTSY Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, BPTSY is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held down by weak financial performance (no revenue, ongoing cash burn, and negative equity), reinforced by bearish technicals with the stock trading below major moving averages. Valuation is also constrained by negative earnings and no dividend support, with only a minor offset from oversold momentum readings.

To see Spark’s full report on BPTSY stock, click here.

More about Biophytis

Biophytis SA is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing drug candidates for age-related diseases, notably its lead small molecule BIO101 (20-hydroxyecdysone). BIO101 is Phase 3-ready for sarcopenia and Phase 2-ready for obesity, with the company headquartered in Paris and subsidiaries in Cambridge, Massachusetts, and Brazil, and its shares listed on Euronext Growth Paris and ADSs on the U.S. OTC market.
